sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain to palpation of the lumbar spine. The patient reports pain in the left hand. The patient reports weakness in their right leg. The patient reports tingling. The patient reports a pop sound immediately after the injury. The patient reports a sweet tooth. The patient reports weight gain. The patient reports intermittent dizziness for one week, worse when standing. He denies chest pain, dyspnea, or syncope. The patient tried ice. The patient tried heat.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's vital signs are normal. The doctor appreciates a 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur. The patient reports decreased range of motion. The patient's right strength is 4 out of 5. The patient's left strength is 5 out of 5. The patient's hemoglobin a1c is 8. The patient's hemoglobin a1c was 8. The patient's hemoglobin a1c is 8. The patient's hemoglobin a1c is 8.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The doctor assesses the patient has an acute lumbar strain. The patient has diabetes. The patient has back pain.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day. The doctor orders physical therapy. The doctor orders an MRI. The doctor wants to see the patient in a couple weeks. The doctor ordered a refill of lisinopril 20 milligrams once a day. The doctor ordered a refill of lisinopril 20 milligrams once a day. The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day. The doctor orders metformin to 1000 milligrams twice daily. The doctor orders an hemoglobin a1c in a couple months.
